pi_items.source: https://incodocs.com/blog/history-of-shipping-container-1956-world-trade/ - Container ships invented 1956
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oil_tanker - VLCC invented 1966
https://www.eia.gov/todayinenergy/detail.php?id=16771 - First LNG carrier 1959
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hovercraft - Hovercraft invented 1956, first operational 1959
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rigid_inflatable_boat - RIBs invented 1964, first commercial 1969
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/High-speed_craft - Fast ferries developed 1990s-2000s
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/History_of_radar - Radar developed 1930s-1940s, not on merchant ships in 1924
https://shippingtandy.com/features/the-jaunty-little-metcalf-motor-coasters/ - Metcalf Motor Coasters had 5 coasters by 1924 in Britain
https://nmmc.co.uk/2024/02/the-history-of-the-magnificent-seven-falmouth-steam-tugs/ - Steam tugs common by 1924, Lynch launched 1924
https://www.providentsailing.co.uk/history-of-provident - Brixham trawler Provident built 1924
https://www.scarboroughsmaritimeheritage.org.uk/article.php?article=126 - Fishing trawlers major industry in 1924
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sailing_at_the_1924_Summer_Olympics - Yachting was established leisure activity by 1924
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brixham_trawler - Sailing trawlers and coasters still common in 1924
https://www.devonhistorysociety.org.uk/wp-content/uploads/2025/02/Tamar-barge-fleet-in-the-1920s-CC.pdf - Barges survived through 1920s, many with steam/motor auxiliary
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steamship - SS Columbus 1924 was flagship steamer, CORDENE tramp steamer built 1924
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pilot_boat - Pilot boats used for channel and port piloting, common in major shipping centers like Solent
Reasonable minimum for major shipping lane (Solent had extensive merchant traffic in 1924)
Historical scenario date - 1924 was steam and sail era in British merchant shipping

https://www.clarity.io/blog/a-timeline-of-londons-air-quality-over-the-years-from-the-great-smog-to-a-clean-air-leader - Coal smoke visibility in 1920s was often 0.5-1 nm in worst cases; 4-5 nm is reasonable average
Bridge Command standard scenario directory structure confirmed in documentation and setup scripts
Standard home directory for user 'ga' as configured in setup scripts

privileged_info_summary: Bridge Command is a real, open-source ship simulator running on Linux. The Solent is a real, major shipping lane between Southampton and Portsmouth. In 1924, the dominant vessel types were coasters, tugs, trawlers, fishing boats, barges, pilot boats, and steamers. Modern vessel types forbidden (container ships 1956, VLCCs 1966, LNG tankers 1959, hovercraft 1956, RIBs 1964, fast ferries 1990s, radar not on merchant ships in 1924) are correctly excluded. Industrial coal haze was prevalent in 1920s Britain, with visibility commonly reduced to 0.5-1 nm in worst cases, making max_visibility 5.0 nm reasonable. Minimum of 6 vessels is appropriate for a major waterway scenario. All metadata values reflect historically accurate requirements for a 1924 Solent simulation.
